title: Cyclosorus gretheri
intro: Cyclosorus gretheri, also known as the Chinese Tuckahoe or Vegetable Lion's Head, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus gretheri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, and it is also used in modern herbal formulations in the form of capsules or tablets to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus gretheri is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its phylogenetic relationships with other ferns.
